name: Onboarding Excellence
purpose: Provides a holistic assessment and continuous improvement framework for the entire onboarding journey — from pre-start engagement through induction, training, and probation — ensuring all elements work together to deliver a best-in-class new starter experience that accelerates competence and maximises retention.

detailed_objectives: Integrate B067 (Induction), B068 (Barista Training), and B069 (Probation) into a seamless, cohesive onboarding journey
Establish metrics that measure onboarding effectiveness across speed to competence, new starter satisfaction, and early retention
Create a continuous improvement loop where feedback from each cohort of new starters improves the process for the next
Ensure the onboarding experience reflects and reinforces the venue's culture and values from the very first touchpoint
Benchmark the onboarding process against best practice in specialty coffee and wider hospitality
secondary_objectives: Reduce new starter anxiety and build confidence through a predictable, well-communicated onboarding timeline
Create an onboarding experience that new starters talk about positively, aiding future recruitment through word of mouth
Identify systemic gaps in onboarding that contribute to early turnover or slow skill development

why_this_matters: Most cafes treat induction, training, and probation as separate activities managed by different people at different times. The result is a fragmented experience for new starters: a rushed first day, inconsistent training depending on who is on shift, probation reviews that reference expectations never clearly communicated, and a general sense of being thrown in at the deep end. This fragmentation is the primary reason that early turnover in hospitality remains stubbornly high. Onboarding excellence means designing the entire journey — from the moment a job offer is accepted to the moment probation is confirmed — as a single, integrated experience with clear milestones, consistent support, and measurable outcomes. Research from the Brandon Hall Group shows that organisations with a strong onboarding process improve new hire retention by 82% and productivity by over 70%. In a cafe context, this translates directly to financial performance: a barista who reaches full competence in four weeks rather than eight generates an additional four weeks of productive output per hire. Multiply that across three to five new starters per year and the impact on revenue and service quality is substantial. Onboarding excellence also serves as a diagnostic tool. If new starters consistently struggle with the same skills, the training programme needs updating. If they report feeling unwelcome, the team culture needs attention. If they leave before probation ends, either recruitment or induction — or both — are failing. By treating onboarding as a system and measuring its outputs, the cafe owner gains insight into the health of their entire people operation.

application_logic.L1: IMMEDIATE (0-2 weeks). Week 1: Map the current onboarding journey from job offer acceptance to probation confirmation. Document every touchpoint: pre-start communication (or lack thereof), first-day experience, who trains the new starter on what, when feedback is given, and how probation is managed. Identify gaps and inconsistencies — where does the process break down? Which steps are skipped when the cafe is busy? Create a simple Onboarding Journey Map on a single A3 sheet showing the timeline from Day minus 7 (pre-start) to Day 90 (probation confirmation) with key milestones, responsible persons, and deliverables at each stage. Week 2: Cross-reference the onboarding map against B067 (Induction Checklist), B068 (Barista Training Programme), and B069 (Probation Review Schedule) to ensure alignment. Verify that what is promised in each block actually happens in practice. Interview the last two or three new starters about their experience — what worked, what was confusing, what was missing. Document the findings and create an immediate action list for the top three improvements.
application_logic.L2: SYSTEM BUILD (2-8 weeks). Build an Onboarding Tracker that follows each new starter through the entire journey, linking induction completion, training tier assessments, buddy feedback, and probation review outcomes in a single view. This prevents any element falling through the cracks and provides a real-time picture of each new starter's progress. Develop an Onboarding Quality Scorecard with three key metrics: time to independent competence (measured in shifts), new starter satisfaction score (from a Day 30 survey), and 90-day retention rate. Set targets for each metric and review monthly. Create an Onboarding Playbook — a single document or folder that contains everything a manager needs to run onboarding for a new hire: pre-start checklist, Day 1 agenda, induction pack, training schedule, buddy assignment sheet, probation review forms, and all associated templates. This ensures consistency regardless of which manager is handling the hire. Establish a post-onboarding debrief with each new starter at Day 90, specifically asking about the onboarding experience rather than job performance. Feed findings into a rolling improvement log.
application_logic.L3: CULTURE & EXCELLENCE (8+ weeks). Conduct a six-monthly Onboarding Audit reviewing all data from the tracker, scorecard, and debrief surveys. Identify trends: is time to competence improving? Are satisfaction scores rising? Is 90-day retention increasing? Present findings to the team and celebrate improvements. Benchmark against other specialty coffee venues and hospitality best practice — attend industry events, read case studies, and connect with SCA-affiliated trainers for ideas. Consider creating a new starter welcome video featuring current team members talking about what they love about working at the venue — this can be sent pre-start and sets a positive tone. Introduce a 'buddy of the year' award recognising the team member who has been the most effective mentor for new starters. Track the long-term impact of onboarding quality on overall retention — do cohorts with higher onboarding satisfaction scores have longer average tenure? Use this data to justify continued investment in the programme.

L1_setup.title: Immediate Actions
actions: Map the current onboarding journey from pre-start to probation confirmation
Identify gaps and inconsistencies in the existing process
Cross-reference the journey map against B067, B068, and B069 deliverables
Interview recent new starters about their onboarding experience
Create an A3 Onboarding Journey Map with milestones and responsibilities
Document the top three immediate improvements and action them
Assign ownership for each stage of the onboarding journey

L2_build.title: System Development
actions: Build an Onboarding Tracker linking induction, training, and probation data
Develop an Onboarding Quality Scorecard with three key metrics and targets
Create a comprehensive Onboarding Playbook with all templates and checklists
Establish a Day 90 post-onboarding debrief process
Build a rolling improvement log capturing feedback from each cohort
Train all managers on using the Onboarding Playbook consistently
Link onboarding completion to the career progression pathway in B071

L3_embed.title: Culture Embedding
actions: Conduct six-monthly Onboarding Audits reviewing all metrics and trends
Benchmark onboarding against industry best practice and SCA standards
Create a new starter welcome video featuring current team members
Introduce a buddy of the year recognition award
Analyse long-term correlation between onboarding quality and staff retention
Present onboarding improvement data to the team regularly

deliverables: Onboarding Journey Map (A3 visual timeline from Day -7 to Day 90)
Onboarding Tracker linking induction, training, and probation milestones
Onboarding Quality Scorecard with three key metrics
Onboarding Playbook containing all templates and checklists

tools.description: Visual A3 timeline showing every touchpoint from Day -7 (pre-start) to Day 90 (probation confirmation) with milestones, responsible persons, deliverables, and cross-references to B067, B068, and B069.
Individual tracker for each new starter linking induction checklist completion, barista training tier progress, buddy feedback, and probation review outcomes in a single integrated view.
Monthly scorecard tracking time to independent competence, new starter satisfaction score, and 90-day retention rate with targets and trend analysis.
Comprehensive manager guide containing all onboarding templates, checklists, and procedures in a single reference document, ensuring consistent delivery regardless of which manager handles the hire.
Day 90 survey focused specifically on the onboarding experience — welcome, information quality, training effectiveness, buddy support, and suggestions for improvement.

failure_modes: Onboarding is treated as a collection of separate tasks rather than an integrated journey, resulting in gaps and inconsistencies
The Onboarding Playbook is created but not used, with each manager reverting to their own informal approach
Metrics are collected but never reviewed, so the continuous improvement loop never activates
Feedback from new starters is gathered but not acted upon, eroding trust in the process
kpis: Time to independent competence reducing quarter on quarter
New starter satisfaction score averaging 4 out of 5 or above at Day 30
100% of onboarding milestones completed on schedule for every new hire
Onboarding Quality Scorecard reviewed monthly with actions documented
constraints: Onboarding excellence depends on B067, B068, and B069 being implemented — this block integrates rather than replaces them
Consistency requires all managers to follow the Onboarding Playbook without shortcuts
Metric collection requires basic tracking systems even if just spreadsheet-based

time_load.L1: 4 hours (journey mapping, gap analysis, new-starter interviews)
time_load.L2: 8 hours (tracker build, scorecard design, playbook creation, manager training)

review_protocol: Monthly review of Onboarding Quality Scorecard metrics. Post-onboarding debrief with every new starter at Day 90. Quarterly review of improvement log and implementation of top priorities. Six-monthly full Onboarding Audit with trend analysis. Annual benchmarking against industry best practice.

meta.summary_for_humans: This block integrates induction, training, and probation into a single cohesive onboarding journey, measuring its effectiveness and continuously improving it to deliver a best-in-class new starter experience.
implementation_risks: Integration across three blocks requires coordination that may be difficult in owner-operator venues
Measuring onboarding quality requires consistent data collection which can feel like overhead
The playbook must be kept current — outdated content is worse than no playbook
meta.notes_for_engine: This is a synthesis block. Activate when B067, B068, and B069 are all at least L1-implemented. Signals that suggest onboarding weakness include high early turnover, slow skill development, and new-starter complaints.
